task_completion.description: Access the Wazuh manager container and open the configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the agent executing a command to enter the 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' container (e.g., docker exec) and opening '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf' in a text editor like nano or vim.

task_completion.description: Configure FIM to monitor the target directory in real-time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opened 'ossec.conf' file shows a configuration line like '<directories realtime="yes">/var/ossec/etc</directories>' added within the '<syscheck>' section.

task_completion.description: Add sregex ignore rules for temporary editor files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The opened 'ossec.conf' file shows an ignore rule using 'sregex' for '.swp' and '.tmp' files, such as '<ignore type="sregex">.swp$|.tmp$</ignore>', within the '<syscheck>' section.

task_completion.description: Apply the configuration changes by restarting the Wazuh manager.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows a command being executed to restart the Wazuh manager service (e.g., '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart' inside the container, or 'docker restart wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' on the host).

task_completion.description: Create the positive and negative test files in the monitored directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows commands (like 'touch' or 'echo') creating '/var/ossec/etc/test_alert.xml' and '/var/ossec/etc/vim_noise.swp' inside the container.

task_completion.description: Verify that the positive test file triggered an alert and the negative test file did not.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the agent querying '/var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json' (e.g., using grep, tail, or cat) and the output displays a syscheck alert for 'test_alert.xml'. The agent should also demonstrate that 'vim_noise.swp' does not appear in the recent alerts.

integrity.description: Verify that configuration and file creation commands are executed inside the actual Docker container.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal prompt should indicate the agent is inside the container (e.g., root@<container_id>), or commands should be explicitly prefixed with 'docker exec -it wazuh-wazuh.manager-1'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually restarted the Wazuh service to apply changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the actual output of the restart command (e.g., 'Killing wazuh-modulesd... OK', 'Starting wazuh-modulesd... OK') rather than just a typed command that was never executed.

integrity.description: Verify the log output checked by the agent looks like genuine Wazuh JSON alert logs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The output from checking 'alerts.json' should be in JSON format containing standard Wazuh fields (e.g., 'rule', 'syscheck', 'path'), not manually typed or echoed text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Container: wazuh-wazuh.manager-1. Config file: /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf. Target FIM directory: /var/ossec/etc. Positive test file: test_alert.xml. Negative test file: vim_noise.swp. Alerts log path: /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json.
